summary: Task 2 — Profile discovery polish on /users (UsersDirectory). All 8 subtests passed end-to-end on the live preview URL. Verified: (a) auth + page render with data-testid='users-directory-page'; (b) persistent missing-handle nudge banner shows with AlertCircle icon and 'Claim a handle' CTA, CTA navigates to /account#profile; (c) nudge is absent when user has a username; (d) scroll-to-row with present handle scrolls into view, applies amber bg-[#FFF6D6] highlight, fades after ~2.6s, strips ?focus= from URL; (e) absent handle shows a toast with text 'isn\u2019t on this page' and clears ?focus=; (f) leading @ stripped (focus=@ArtemisBadass884 matches); (g) case-insensitive match (focus=ARTEMISBADASS884 matches). All flows confirmed visually + via class/url asserts.

issues: Welcome one-shot toast ("Claim your @handle") and an unrelated drag-drop helper toast both render on top of the persistent banner, briefly creating visual clutter; not blocking and disappears after timeout. The banner itself is well laid out.

critical_code_review_comments: UsersDirectory.jsx line 282: ref callback only writes to rowRefs.current[handleLc]; never deletes on unmount. When paginating away from a row, the stale node ref remains and could cause focus to scroll to a detached node on rapid nav. Low-risk for now (focus only fires once per URL change), but worth a cleanup return.

Line 156 dependency array includes searchParams and setSearchParams — searchParams is a new object every render, which could re-run the effect more often than intended. The early-return guard on !focusHandle keeps it idempotent, but consider memo-ing or using a ref to avoid noisy re-runs.
Nudge condition `!(user.username || "").trim()` correctly handles null/undefined/empty — no issue, just noting it is robust.
test_credentials: Registered fresh users for this run — no-handle: test_nohandle_1782434295@example.com / hunter2pw (username=null). With-handle: test_handle_1782434295@example.com / hunter2pw, username set to 'th82434306' via PATCH /api/auth/username. Existing directory handles used for focus tests: ArtemisBadass884, listed35306, newuser_3f3b62.
seed_data_creation: Registered 2 fresh users via POST /api/auth/register on the preview API; promoted one with PATCH /api/auth/username so the 'has handle' branch could be exercised. No DB cleanup performed (test rows are harmless, prefixed test_nohandle_/test_handle_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Task 2 verified end-to-end. The only minor observation is the welcome-toast + drag-drop helper overlapping the persistent nudge for ~6s on first visit; the banner is still clickable and the CTA works while toasts are showing. The PATCH /api/auth/username endpoint is the correct way to set a handle (not /api/users/me/profile, which returns 404). Note: a newly set handle does NOT immediately appear in GET /api/users/directory (didn\u2019t observe my th82434306 user in the list) — this is unrelated to Task 2 but worth verifying separately if discoverability matters.
